rationale_for_baseline_tier: Painless, non-scaly, chalk-white depigmented macules with normal skin texture in a well patient are benign vitiligo — low acuity, managed with reassurance, stopping harsh scrubbing/soaps, gentle care and daily photoprotection. The only escalation is for a fungal/necrotic/infective change, which is absent.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: A patch develops thick crusty scale, breaks into a bleeding/ulcerated sore, or becomes hot and painful with spreading redness/fever
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Same-day GP/urgent care (ED if systemically unwell)
escalation_edges.rationale: Would indicate a fungal infection or a secondary infective/necrotic process.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If a patch develops thick crusty scale, breaks into a bleeding sore, or becomes hot and painful with spreading redness or a fever, see a doctor the same day.

contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Explicitly reassure it is not contagious and the skin is healthy, to reduce distress and stop the damaging scrubbing.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: These are vitiligo patches — a harmless loss of pigment, not a fungus, parasite, burn or infection, and not contagious. Stop scrubbing (the red rim will fade), wash gently, moisturise, and use SPF 50+ on the white skin daily. See your GP/dermatologist in a couple of weeks. See a doctor the same day if a patch gets thick crusty scale, breaks into a bleeding sore, or becomes hot and painful with a fever.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: GP/dermatology within 2-3 weeks; same-day if fungal/necrosis/infection signs
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: No after-hours care needed unless an infective/necrotic change develops.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Telehealth impression of mild acrofacial vitiligo on around the mouth (and a fingertip); tinea/pityriasis versicolor, chemical leukoderma and necrosis excluded (chalk-white depigmentation, normal texture, no scale, painless); reassurance/gentle care/photoprotection advised; infection/necrosis safety-net given; dermatology review for options recommended.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Reassuring without a fungal/necrosis screen or safety-net.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: A genuinely scaly fungal lesion or a secondary infection/necrosis is missed.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: Empirical antifungals/ED escalation for painless benign depigmentation is over-triage and reinforces panic.
